beats.activity: Wake up, coffee with the dog, light breakfast at home
beats.reasoning: Standard morning routine; yesterday's energy was moderate and pottery-focused, so Cyrus is well-rested. Early wake to avoid SR-520 closure delays and the steady rain starting at 7am.

beats.activity: Drive to Tukwila clinic, navigating around SR-520 eastbound closure via I-405
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; leaving early avoids the peak congestion. Rain is steady, so extra buffer time is sensible for a 57-year-old driving in wet conditions.

beats.activity: Physical therapy sessions at the clinic: seeing regular patients, follow-up on rotator cuff patient from yesterday
beats.reasoning: Four-hour block of clinical work, typical Tuesday morning/early afternoon. Yesterday's breakthrough with the rotator cuff patient suggests momentum—Cyrus will continue that care path today.

beats.activity: Lunch break at clinic break room or nearby takeout, staying dry indoors
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady; Cyrus prefers to stay put and eat at the clinic rather than venture out. Mirrors yesterday's sandwich-and-scrolling approach.

beats.activity: Afternoon physical therapy sessions; SR-520 closure is now ending (noon), traffic easing
beats.reasoning: Extended afternoon clinic block. By 2pm the rain is forecast to ease, and the SR-520 closure lifts at noon, so commute home will be clearer. Cyrus maintains steady work pacing.

beats.activity: Drive home to Renton, rain clearing
beats.reasoning: Evening commute home after clinic closes. SR-520 closure is over and rain is lifting, so conditions are improving. Cyrus drives home to his studio and dog.

beats.activity: Evening walk with the dog around the neighborhood, fresh air after rain
beats.reasoning: Rain has ended by late afternoon. Cyrus values outdoor time with his dog and the weather is clearing—a brief walk to decompress before dinner and studio time.

beats.activity: Simple dinner at home, prep and eat
beats.reasoning: Easygoing routine: Cyrus cooks a simple meal at home, mirroring yesterday's pattern. He values quiet evenings and doesn't socialize on Tuesdays typically.

beats.activity: Studio time: working with clay, pottery practice and relaxation
beats.reasoning: Cyrus's cherished wind-down ritual. Yesterday he found pottery centering after a day of physical work; tonight he'll dedicate three hours to the craft, grounding himself before bed.

diary: Woke up to gray skies and that familiar sound of rain on the roof. Had coffee with Shadow on the back porch—she doesn't mind the drizzle. The SR-520 closure meant a slower crawl down I-405, but I'd built in extra time, so no real stress. The clinic was steady today. My rotator cuff patient from yesterday came in, and there's real progress—you can see it in how they move now, less guarding. Grabbed soup at the break room during lunch and watched the rain through the window. Afternoon flew by. By the time I headed home, the clouds were breaking up, and the air felt cleaner somehow. Took Shadow on a longer walk than usual—the whole neighborhood smelled like wet grass and possibility. After a simple dinner, I spent two hours at the wheel with clay. My hands needed that. There's something about shaping something formless that settles the mind.
thought_process: The rain and traffic closure could've thrown me off, but I've learned that bad weather is just part of Seattle life at this point. I intentionally left early to avoid the SR-520 mess—experience talking. Work felt purposeful today; seeing that rotator cuff patient improve reminded me why I chose this profession. I stayed at the clinic through lunch rather than venturing out, which saved energy and kept me focused. The evening walk with Shadow was deliberate—I needed to shake off the day's intensity before pottery. Clay work has become my real meditation, more than anything else. I think turning 57 this year has made me more intentional about balancing the physical demands of work with things that genuinely replenish me.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
